text:
REGIME — Broad risk-on grind, defensives led, metals lagged, vol compressed.

INDICES — VIX 24.2, falling. QQQ +0.60%, IWM +0.43%, SPY +0.47%, DIA +0.37%. Small caps (IWM +0.43%) underperformed large cap tech (QQQ +0.60%). VIXY dropped -2.45%, the sharpest cross-asset move of the session, confirming fear unwind.

SECTORS — XLP +0.94%, XLY +0.82%, XLE +0.73%, XLF +0.71%, XLK +0.58%, XLI +0.51%, XLRE +0.36%, XLC +0.05%, XLV -0.36%, XLU -0.37%, XLB -0.38%. Top industries: MSOS +6.23%, IBIT +4.08%. Bottom industries: TAN -1.26%, LIT -1.42%.

THE 7s — Defense +1.25%, Fab +1.22%, Housing +1.08%, Toll Road +1.00%, Semi +0.84%, Bank +0.73%, Industrial +0.66%, Energy +0.57%, New Staples +0.51%, Old Staples +0.45%, Insurance +0.44%, Freight +0.35%, Cloud +0.19%, Old Metals +0.05%, Medtech -0.16%, Power -0.27%, Health -0.39%, Buildout -0.40%, Software -0.63%, New Metals -0.85%. Strongest ticker in Defense 7 not specified in data; weakest in New Metals 7 not individually named — GLD proxy confirmed weakest cross-asset at -0.41%.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 54.4, Neutral. Top movers: MSOS +6.23% (Industries), IBIT +4.08% (Industries), WGMI +2.63% (Industries). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +6.19%, worst XLY -4.53%; best basket Energy +8.57%, worst Software -10.72%. Ceasefire framework with Iran lifted sentiment despite NFP 178k stoking inflation fears via Brent at $121.88.

WATCH — EATZ +2.02%, 10.4x average volume, extreme vol spike warrants monitoring for continuation or reversal. IJK +0.28%, 8.0x vol on minimal price move signals positioning activity. IJS +0.50%, 6.2x vol with small-cap value diverging from IWM trend.
